serviceLogs.notes: Replace 2 GFCIs garage, 2 duplex receptacles, 2 exterior GFCIs, fix polarity half-hot switch living room.
Install receptacle box at stove, remove fluorescent fixture, lay out 2 new 6" recessed lights + 1 for sink.
Troubleshot laundry circuit overload. Convert Nema 10-30 for 2 standard receptacles. Independent dryer and washer circuits.
Remove 3 receptacles for cabinet removal. Troubleshoot garage circuit, low voltage light, barbecue receptacle. Remove under-cabinet lighting.
Install 4 LV lights w/transformer and box. Trench and hide wire. Install clips for barbecue receptacle. Replace 13 landscape lights.
Install 6 new 4" recessed lights and replace 6 x 6" recessed can lights for clip recessed lights.
Remove 2 receptacles and reroute circuit for new ceiling hood range receptacle and new receptacle above new counter.
Replace 1 dimmer and 1 standard switch, troubleshoot lights flickering.
[Demo] 2nd Ohase of Demo. 90% Completed. Started Drywall Cut Channels for new Box Layout and Wiring
[Planning] Started marking some of the hair station receptacle boxing layout
[Rough-in] Started installing receptacle boxing layout and attic emt raceway box layout
[Rough-in] This log is the summary of all previous work logs. Active logging will start from here.
[Rough-in] Started main EMT raceway layout, installed 100 feet of conduit and finish 2 out of 5 junction boxes

conditions.situation: Any single day profit < 50% of daily target

actions.value: Job size, Mileage, Material cost
Do not change pricing mid-week. Log the cause and continue execution.
What made this day weak? Identify the dominant cost driver.

conditions.situation: Any day profit ≥ 150–200% of daily target

actions.value: Flag that job as reference. Compare future jobs against it.
Use this job as a benchmark for pricing, scope size, travel radius, and customer type.
What made this job structurally strong?

conditions.situation: Mileage cost exceeds 15% of quoted price

actions.value: Should this job have been priced higher or bundled differently?
Bundle similar jobs geographically or add a trip minimum charge.
Adjust future pricing rules for distance, add trip minimums.

conditions.expression: 3+ service jobs in a row below target
conditions.situation: 3+ service jobs in a row below target profit

actions.value: Are service jobs supporting overhead or consuming capacity?
Raise minimum service pricing, enforce scope boundaries, or shift to project leads.
Pause accepting similar service jobs for 48 hours.
Is weekly performance dependent on a small number of outsized wins?
Increase consistency by improving average job quality.
Mark the week as fragile. Do nothing operationally.

conditions.situation: Estimated profit < minimum acceptable daily fraction

actions.value: Is this job capable of contributing meaningfully to daily profit?
Decline, reprice, or expand scope before acceptance.
Can this job mathematically meet the minimum profit floor?

conditions.situation: Material cost is a high percentage of quoted price

actions.value: Does this job rely too heavily on pass-through material with limited margin?
Increase markup, charge procurement fee, or decline low-margin material-heavy work.
What is the actual labor margin after material pass-through?

conditions.situation: Price resistance, scope creep signals, or urgency without clarity

actions.value: Does this customer increase execution or payment risk?
Tighten terms, require deposit, or decline before engagement.
Communication quality and expectation alignment.
